name: Peking Union Medical College Hospital
shortDescription: Top-ranked Chinese teaching hospital; flagship of the Republican-era American medical tradition and a major academic trial site, including for rare-disease research.
narrativeHook: Built with Rockefeller Foundation money and opened to patients in 1921 as a self-described 'Johns Hopkins of China,' PUMCH remains the most-cited teaching hospital in the country and a canonical lineage point for China's first internationally-trained physician cohort.

hospitalContext.affiliation: Chinese Academy of Medical Sciences / Peking Union Medical College (CAMS-PUMC system)
hospitalContext.hookFact: Has topped the Fudan University Hospital Management Institute's 'China's Best Hospital Rankings' every year since the index was first published in 2010 — a streak that reached 13 consecutive years with the 2021 edition (released November 2022).

timeline.status: Peking Union Medical College established 1917 with Rockefeller China Medical Board funding; the teaching hospital opened to the public in September 1921 on the site of a former Qing palace, modelled on Johns Hopkins.
timeline.note: Final Rockefeller outlay reached roughly $7.5M, far above the original $1–1.5M budget.

timeline.status: Operating as the clinical arm of the Chinese Academy of Medical Sciences / Peking Union Medical College (CAMS-PUMC) system.
Major academic trial site as China entered the GCP era; later host to the National Rare Diseases Registry System (NRDRS) led by PUMCH.
By 2024 operating >2,000 inpatient beds across multiple campuses with >130,000 annual discharges; ranked No. 1 in the National Tertiary Public Hospital Performance Evaluation.

name: Fudan University Shanghai Cancer Center
nameZh: 复旦大学附属肿瘤医院
shortDescription: China's oldest cancer specialty hospital and a Grade-3A teaching hospital of Fudan University's Shanghai Medical College; consistently a top-three oncology specialty on the Fudan Hospital Rankings, and a primary China site for PD-1 and ADC registration trials.
narrativeHook: Founded on 1 March 1931 as the Sino-Belgian Radium Institute, FUSCC was the first cancer-specialty hospital in China; by the 2020s it was hosting the China leg of registration-grade oncology trials for the first wave of domestically-discovered checkpoint inhibitors and ADCs — including the camrelizumab CameL (NCT03134872) and sintilimab ORIENT-12 (NCT03629925) Phase 3 NSCLC programs and FUSCC-led trials evaluating disitamab vedotin and ivonescimab combinations.

hospitalContext.affiliation: Affiliated with Shanghai Medical College, Fudan University (renamed FUSCC in 2002 after Shanghai Medical University merged into Fudan in 2000; previously the Cancer Hospital of Shanghai First Medical College / Shanghai Medical University from 1954).

keyTrials.role: One of 52 China sites in the Phase 3 first-line non-squamous NSCLC registration trial that supported camrelizumab's NMPA NSCLC approval.
FUSCC Department of Radiation Oncology contributed as a site in the 42-center Phase 3 squamous NSCLC trial.
Single-center Phase 2 in immunomodulatory triple-negative breast cancer; PI Zhi-Ming Shao / Xichun Hu — flagship of FUSCC's FUTURE-series precision-subtyping trials.

keyTrials.trial: Disitamab vedotin + ivonescimab in cisplatin-ineligible MIBC
keyTrials.role: FUSCC Department of Urology Phase Ib/II combining a domestic HER2 ADC with a domestic PD-1/VEGF bispecific in muscle-invasive bladder cancer.

hospitalContext.hookFact: Ranked #3 in the oncology specialty on the Fudan University Hospital Management Institute's Chinese Hospital Specialty Reputation Rankings — behind CAMS Cancer Hospital and Sun Yat-sen University Cancer Center — a position it has held continuously since the rankings began in 2010.

timeline.status: Founded 1 March 1931 as the Sino-Belgian Radium Institute (中比镭锭治疗院) inside Shanghai's Sacred Heart Hospital — the first cancer-specialty institution in China; became the independent Shanghai Radium Institute in 1936.

Operated as the Cancer Hospital of Shanghai First Medical College / Shanghai Medical University from 1954; renamed Fudan University Shanghai Cancer Center in 2002 following the 2000 merger of Shanghai Medical University into Fudan University.
China site for the first generation of multi-center oncology Phase 2/3 trials as China entered the GCP/ICH-aligned era.
Site in Phase 3 registration trials for the PD-1 wave (camrelizumab CameL, sintilimab ORIENT-series); FUSCC's own FUTURE / FUTURE-C-Plus precision-subtyping breast-cancer trials begin enrolling.

Site for ADC, bispecific, and CDK-program trials; Department of Urology runs investigator-initiated combinations of disitamab vedotin (RC48) with ivonescimab; ~2,149 beds and >2 million outpatient visits annually as of end-2023.
